item.title: High Rates of Seroprotection for Measles in Pediatric Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - The ongoing U.S. measles outbreak raises concerns for immunosuppressed children with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). We evaluated measles seroprotection in pediatric IBD patients using a high-sen...

item.title: Clinical impact of toxin detection in children with PCR-confirmed Clostridioides difficile infection.
item.description: European journal of pediatrics - Observational study - The diagnosis of Clostridioides difficile infection (CDI) in children is challenging due to high rates of asymptomatic colonization and the limited ability of available assays to differentiate colo...

item.title: Serum thiol redox-related alterations in asymptomatic first-degree relatives are associated with future Crohn's disease.
item.description: Redox biology - Increased oxidative stress is well documented in patients with Crohn's disease (CD). However, it remains unclear whether early redox-related alterations occur prior to diagnosis and whether they ar...

item.title: A Rare Case of Sterile Retrostyloid Abscess in a Patient With Crohn's Disease: Diagnostic and Therapeutic Insights.
item.description: Case reports in medicine - Aseptic abscess syndrome is a rare inflammatory disorder of unclear etiology, characterized by sterile lesions densely infiltrated by polymorphonuclear neutrophils. Its frequently associated with i...

item.title: Tryptophan pathway metabotypes associate with disease activity and immune-metabolic dysfunction in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: medRxiv : the preprint server for health sciences - Tryptophan (Trp) metabolism is a central immunometabolic axis in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and has been linked to inflammatory activity and immune regulation. While individual Trp metabolite...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease phenotypes in diverse populations: a global comparative analysis.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - The aim of this study was to determine if Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC) manifest similarly in different parts of the world. Investigators of cohort studies from around the world ...

item.title: All roads lead to NF-κB: the NF-κB pathway as a major target for intestinal inflammatory disorders.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - This review aims to comprehensively examine the role of the NF-κB signalling pathway as a central mediator of intestinal inflammation, integrating evidence from microbiota dysbiosis, immune activat...
